subject: [RFC PATCH 40/45] PM: hibernate: walk per-superpageblock free lists in mark_free_pages

2026-04-30.patch_summary: This patch modifies the mark_free_pages function in the kernel's power management code to correctly handle free pages after the introduction of superpage blocks (SPBs). The existing function walked the buddy allocator's free lists, but since SPBs were introduced, free pages now live on per-superpageblock free lists. This patch adds a loop to iterate over each superpage block and its corresponding free list, ensuring that all free pages are correctly marked as such during hibernation. If a zone has no superpage blocks (e.g., an unpopulated hotplug zone), the function falls back to using the zone-level free list. This change improves the accuracy of the hibernation snapshot and prevents unnecessary memory usage.
